markets.question: Will the Republicans win the Maine Senate race in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 midterm Maine U.S. Senate election, inclusive of any run-offs.

A candidate shall be considered to represent a party in the event that he or she is the nominee of the party in question. Candidates other than the Democratic or Republican nominee (e.g., Greens, Libertarian, independent) may be added at a later date.

Candidates who run as independents will not be encompassed by the “Democrat” or “Republican” options regardless of any affiliation they may have with the party.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on the official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polling in the Maine Senate race shows Democratic primary frontrunner Graham Platner holding modest leads over incumbent Republican Susan Collins, reflecting the state's left-leaning electorate and Collins's mixed favorability readings in multiple surveys conducted through April 2026. Janet Mills's withdrawal from the Democratic primary in late April consolidated support behind Platner, an oyster farmer and veteran, ahead of the June 9 primary. Traders' consensus at 73.5 percent for a Democratic outcome captures these trends alongside historical partisan patterns in Maine, while the 26.5 percent Republican share accounts for Collins's long incumbency and bipartisan record. The November general election and primary results remain key variables that could adjust probabilities based on turnout and further campaign developments.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

This market will resolve to "Infinite" if Infinite win the match against Imperial Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Will Russia enter Druzkhivka by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Druzkhivka (48.61631417285371° N, 37.528161462381036° E; https://maps.app.goo.gl/cH9ekgbf9qbxGPUk6) by December 31, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET.

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the city or settlement is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

markets.question: Will Silver (SI) hit (HIGH) $120 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Silver (SI) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy approaches this Exort Series Main Stage BO3 with the clear edge in recent European CS2 form, having posted an undefeated Swiss stage record built on disciplined map control and academy-level coordination backed by Team Spirit's infrastructure. Wave Esports enters after a winless group run marked by inconsistent execution across multiple maps and limited depth in their current roster, leaving them exposed against opponents who rotate young prospects effectively. Head-to-head trends in comparable C-tier events favor academy squads with stronger map pools and recent practice emphasis, while the online format eliminates travel variables but keeps individual player availability and last-minute lineup tweaks as the primary swing factors for series outcomes.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Hong Kong’s May precipitation shows notable dispersion across rainfall bins because the southwest monsoon’s onset timing, combined with variable convective activity and sea-surface temperatures in the South China Sea, can swing monthly totals by 50–100 mm. The Hong Kong Observatory’s seasonal outlook calls for normal to above-normal rainfall through July, driven by prevailing moisture transport and ENSO-neutral conditions that historically support 14–15 rainy days and totals near the 305 mm climatological average. With roughly half the month remaining, short-term model runs and any early-season tropical systems will determine whether totals cluster in the lower or mid-range bins; traders are therefore weighting the risk of suppressed monsoon flow against the potential for enhanced thunderstorm clusters.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the CO-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the CO-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will Marco Rubio win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

events.title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
events.description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the 2028 presidential election favors Vice President JD Vance at 18.6 percent, California Governor Gavin Newsom at 16.7 percent, and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 14.1 percent, reflecting their elevated national profiles in a post-2024 landscape still more than two years from primaries. Recent White House momentum has lifted Rubio through foreign policy visibility on issues involving Iran and Venezuela, while an April poll showed a modest dip in Republican preferences for Vance. Newsom maintains steady positioning via his term-limited role and Democratic primary exposure. The contest remains closely matched absent decisive endorsements, 2026 midterm outcomes, or major early primary polling shifts, leaving room for cabinet performance or unexpected developments to widen gaps among the field.

markets.question: Will Brian Armstrong appear on the UpOnly podcast by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if a new episode of the UpOnly podcast featuring the specified guest is publicly released by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

“Released” means the full episode is published and available to the general public on an official UpOnly platform (e.g. YouTube, Spotify, Apple Podcasts, or the show’s official feed).

Previews, clips, teasers, or promotional material do not count toward resolution.

The resolution source will be the official UpOnly podcast channels and/or a consensus of credible public reporting.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the MN-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MN-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: The open seat in Minnesota's 2nd Congressional District, created by Angie Craig's April 2025 decision to run for Senate instead of reelection, has drawn a competitive Democratic primary field ahead of the August 11, 2026 contest. Trader consensus heavily favors the eventual Democratic nominee at 88.5% because the district carries a Cook Partisan Voting Index of D+3, reflecting consistent Democratic performance in recent presidential cycles and a structural advantage in suburban and exurban areas. Recent developments, including Matt Little's strong showing at the May 2026 DFL convention, have clarified the Democratic side without shifting the general-election outlook. Republicans, led by state Senator Eric Pratt, face steeper fundraising and turnout hurdles that align with the 20.5% implied probability for their nominee.

events.title: RC Celta de Vigo vs. Sevilla FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming La Liga game, scheduled for Saturday, May 23, 2026 between RC Celta de Vigo and Sevilla FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Celta Vigo’s stronger mid-table position and solid home record at Balaídos underpin trader consensus around a 55.5% implied probability for the home side. After 37 La Liga matchdays, Celta sit sixth with 51 points and have collected points in recent fixtures against Atletico Madrid and others, while Sevilla remain 13th with 43 points amid inconsistent results and defensive vulnerabilities. Multiple absences on both sides—including Celta’s Carl Starfelt and Sevilla’s Marcão—add uncertainty, yet the hosts’ recent head-to-head edge and favorable schedule spot continue to shape market pricing toward a narrow home advantage with draw odds holding steady near 25.5%.

markets.question: Set 1 Winner: Rodionov vs Lajovic
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Jurij Rodionov and Dusan Lajovic in the Roland Garros, Qualification ATP, originally scheduled for May 19, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to “Rodionov” if Jurij Rodionov wins the first set. It will resolve to “Lajovic” if Dusan Lajovic wins the first set.
If the match begins but is not completed, and the first set is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on that completed set. If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Grand Slam match statistics.
markets.groupItemTitle: Roland Garros, Qualification ATP: Jurij Rodionov vs Dusan Lajovic Set 1 Winner

events.title: Roland Garros, Qualification ATP: Jurij Rodionov vs Dusan Lajovic
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Jurij Rodionov and Dusan Lajovic in the Roland Garros, Qualification ATP, originally scheduled for May 19, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Jurij Rodionov' if Jurij Rodionov advances against Dusan Lajovic.

This market will resolve to 'Dusan Lajovic' if Dusan Lajovic advances against Jurij Rodionov.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jurij Rodionov and Dusan Lajovic meet for the first time in the opening round of Roland Garros qualifying on clay. Lajovic brings greater experience on the surface, including prior main-draw appearances at the French Open and a career built around consistent clay-court results, while currently ranked 136. Rodionov, ranked near 158, has shown recent form on clay through challenger events but enters with limited Grand Slam qualifying success. Both players arrive after mixed results in European spring events, with no reported injuries or withdrawals altering the draw. The match timing on the first day of qualifying adds standard variables such as early-round adaptation to Paris conditions and court speed.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will US withdraw from NATO by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Donald Trump’s repeated public criticisms of NATO allies for insufficient support in the Iran conflict and related Strait of Hormuz operations have intensified trader focus on potential U.S. withdrawal, alongside the Pentagon’s May 2026 order to remove 5,000 troops from Germany over the following six to twelve months. These moves reflect ongoing force posture reviews and longstanding administration concerns over European defense spending and burden-sharing. However, Section 1250A of the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act bars any presidential suspension, termination, or withdrawal from the North Atlantic Treaty without Senate advice and consent or separate congressional legislation, creating a significant procedural hurdle. NATO members are monitoring the situation while preparing for possible reduced U.S. leadership, including adjustments to future summit schedules, as traders weigh the gap between rhetorical signals and the legal requirements for formal exit.

markets.question: Will Ralph Rebandt win the 2026 Michigan Governor Republican primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Michigan, scheduled to take place on August 4, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Michigan Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Michigan Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Michigan Governor Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Michigan, scheduled to take place on August 4, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Michigan Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Michigan Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: John James and Perry Johnson remain the clear frontrunners in Michigan’s Republican primary for governor ahead of the August 4 vote, with the race staying tight amid mutual legal challenges over petition signatures and campaign practices. Johnson, a self-funding businessman, has poured millions into television advertising while accusing James of deceptive branding that suggests incumbency, prompting a lawsuit in Ingham County court. James, the sitting U.S. representative, holds a modest edge in recent polling but faces counter-challenges from Johnson-aligned groups alleging invalid signatures on his filings. These procedural disputes and Johnson’s heavy spending have kept the contest competitive, with other declared candidates such as Mike Cox and Aric Nesbitt trailing well behind and showing little momentum to close the gap before primary day.

eventMetadata.context_description: Everett Jackson leads the Republican primary for Texas's 30th congressional district with an 88.3% implied probability, reflecting his top finish in the March 3 primary where he secured 38% of the vote and advanced to the May 26 runoff against Sholdon Daniels. Jackson's stronger showing among the four candidates, combined with ongoing early voting that began May 18, has reinforced trader consensus on his path to the nomination. Gregor Heise and Nils Walker, who each received under 20% in March, trail at 6.5% and 0.3% as eliminated contenders. The district's heavily Democratic tilt in the general election has kept focus on primary dynamics, where Jackson's plurality and campaign positioning provide the clearest edge ahead of the runoff outcome.
